name: Text Flipping Board
summary: A split-flap display board with 6x22 character grid showing animated flipping text with accent color highlights.
detailedDescription: Text Flipping Board recreates vintage airport/train station split-flap displays using CSS animations. The 6-row x 22-column grid renders each character as individual FlapCell component. Characters cycle through FLAP_CHARS set (A-Z, 0-9, symbols) before landing on target. Each cell has top/bottom flap halves that rotate along horizontal axis, creating mechanical flip illusion. Accent colors (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, violet, white) randomly highlight 20% of scramble frames. Timing uses staggered delays: 30ms per column, 20ms per row, 55ms per step. Flip duration is 350ms per character. Text size clamps from 6px to 22px based on viewport. This creates nostalgic, mechanical text transition effect ideal for departure boards, retro aesthetics, or any context where physical flip animation adds character and visual interest beyond digital transitions.
purpose: Animate text with realistic flip transitions
Add accent color highlights during animation
behavior: Staggers timing by row and column
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
